root_text.sn22.21:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.21:1.4: Katamesānaṁ kho, bhante, dhammānaṁ nirodho ‘nirodho’ti vuccatī”ti?
root_text.sn22.21:1.5: “Rūpaṁ kho, ānanda, aniccaṁ saṅkhataṁ paṭiccasamuppannaṁ khayadhammaṁ vayadhammaṁ virāgadhammaṁ nirodhadhammaṁ.
root_text.sn22.21:1.7: Vedanā aniccā saṅkhatā paṭiccasamuppannā khayadhammā vayadhammā virāgadhammā nirodhadhammā.
root_text.sn22.21:1.10: saṅkhārā aniccā saṅkhatā paṭiccasamuppannā khayadhammā vayadhammā virāgadhammā nirodhadhammā.
root_text.sn22.21:1.12: Viññāṇaṁ aniccaṁ saṅkhataṁ paṭiccasamuppannaṁ khayadhammaṁ vayadhammaṁ virāgadhammaṁ nirodhadhammaṁ.
root_text.sn22.21:1.14: Imesaṁ kho, ānanda, dhammānaṁ nirodho ‘nirodho’ti vuccatī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.21:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn22.21:1.4: The cessation of what things does this refer to?”
translation_text.sn22.21:1.5: “Ānanda, form is impermanent, conditioned, dependently originated, liable to end, vanish, fade away, and cease.
translation_text.sn22.21:1.6: Its cessation is what ‘cessation’ refers to.
translation_text.sn22.21:1.12: Consciousness is impermanent, conditioned, dependently originated, liable to end, vanish, fade away, and cease.
translation_text.sn22.21:1.13: Its cessation is what ‘cessation’ refers to.
translation_text.sn22.21:1.14: When they speak of ‘cessation’, its the cessation of these things that this refers to.”
